test_cases.failure_message:
Timed out after 1500.000s.
The function passed to Eventually failed at /home/prow/go/src/sigs.k8s.io/cluster-api-provider-azure/test/e2e/aks_byo_node.go:175 with:
Expected
    <int32>: 3
to equal
    <int32>: 5
test_cases.failure_body:
[FAILED] Timed out after 1500.000s.
The function passed to Eventually failed at /home/prow/go/src/sigs.k8s.io/cluster-api-provider-azure/test/e2e/aks_byo_node.go:175 with:
Expected
    <int32>: 3
to equal
    <int32>: 5
In [It] at: /home/prow/go/src/sigs.k8s.io/cluster-api-provider-azure/test/e2e/aks_byo_node.go:183 @ 06/09/26 16:48:58.64

ai_summary.summary: Workload cluster creation failed due to transient Azure API connectivity issues preventing node pool reconciliation

ai_analysis.root_cause: The test failure occurred because CAPI controller could not connect to the workload cluster API server to reconcile MachinePools, as indicated by repeated "connection to the workload cluster is down" errors in the capi-controller-manager logs for all three node pools (pool0, pool1, pool2) of the capz-e2e-7fm1xe-aks cluster. This connectivity issue was caused by underlying Azure infrastructure problems, specifically "We've temporarily lost connection to the health metrics of this AKS cluster" errors reported in the capz-controller-manager logs for the AzureManagedControlPlane resource. The KCP controller logs also show repeated DNS lookup failures for the cluster API endpoint with "no such host" errors, confirming the connectivity issues were at the Azure infrastructure level. These Azure API connectivity issues prevented the test from verifying that all expected nodes (5) were ready, causing the Eventually assertion to timeout after 1500s when only 3 nodes were reported as ready. The MachinePool resources themselves show they were successfully created and are in Running phase with Ready conditions, indicating the underlying infrastructure was provisioned correctly once connectivity was restored.
ai_analysis.suggested_fix: No remediation possible from available evidence: The failure was caused by transient Azure API connectivity issues that resolved automatically. The test should be retried. If this pattern persists, investigate Azure API throttling or network connectivity from the test environment to Azure.
